other_observers: none; I was not the original finder of this bird. Its presence had previously been reported on cobirds.
description: It was a short-tailed warbler, moderately active.  The upperparts were a uniform gray, with white wingbars and some white feather edgings.  It had a large black face patch, with a prominent white supercilium separating black below from gray above.  It had a large, white ear patch behind the black face patch.  A broken white eye ring appeared to be more prominent behind the eye.  The throat was a bright, unstreaked yellow, changing abruptly to white at the breast.  The rest of the underparts were white, with dingy streaking at the sides. This streaking was most prominent on the upper breast, and to a lesser extent on the undertail coverts.  It was giving a single call note, and was foraging in scotch pine trees.Behaviors: Foraging fairly actively in pine trees.Call: single call note, given repeatedlyPlumage: uncertain
